description: Renders a shaded inequality region on a 2D graph. Supports single inequalities and compound inequality conditions using <, >, <=, >=.
utility: Plot a shaded inequality region on a 2D graph

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph container to plot on

arguments.name: inequality
arguments.description: Inequality expression as a quoted string using <, >, <=, >=. Supports compound conditions separated by commas - e.g., "y >= sin(x), y <= sin(x) + 1"

arguments.name: fillOpacity
arguments.description: Fill opacity for the shaded region - e.g., fo(0.3)

arguments.name: fillColor
arguments.description: Fill color for the shaded region - e.g., fc(yellow)

alternates.description: Shade the region above the curve y = f(x)
Shaded inequality region with custom color and opacity

alternates.syntax: inequalityplot(G, "y >= f(x), y <= g(x)", c(blue), fo(0.3))
alternates.description: Shade the region between two curves

examples.scenario: Shaded band between two sine curves
examples.expression:
G5 = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-3, 3), type(plotting))
inequalityplot(G5, "y >= sin(x), y <= sin(x) + 1", c(orange), fo(0.3))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-2, 10), type(plotting))
inequalityplot(G, "y < x^2 + 1", c(blue), fo(0.3))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
inequalityplot(G, "y >= 0", c(green), fo(0.2))
